title: 2016-2022 Lexus RX350 Spark Plug Replacement Instructions
description: Replace spark plugs on 2016-2022 Lexus RX350, including intake manifold removal to access the rear three plugs on the V6 engine.
tips: Use a small flat-blade screwdriver under the MAF sensor connector tab to help release stubborn connectors.
Remove the air filter out of its box before doing any major work to avoid dropping dirt on top of it.
Clean the throttle body while it's off — spray brake cleaner onto a rag, open the throttle plate with your finger, and wipe the interior.
Check the throttle body O-ring gasket; if it sticks to the throttle body, reseat it in its groove. Replace only if damaged or deformed.
Blow off accumulated dirt from the intake manifold surface before removal so debris doesn't fall into the intake runners.
Plug or cover the intake runner openings with a rag after manifold removal to prevent bolts, nuts, or dirt from falling in.
Inspect the upper-to-lower manifold O-ring gaskets — replace if cracked or dry; Lexus recommends replacing them every time.
You don't need to mark ignition coils before removal — they are all identical and interchangeable.
Anti-seize on spark plug threads is not necessary; these plugs rarely seize.
If you don't have a torque wrench for the spark plugs, tighten until the gasket crushes and resistance increases, then add a small amount more.
For rear spark plugs, use a combination of ~3-inch extensions; an electric or air ratchet speeds this up significantly.
Install all intake manifold bolts and nuts finger-tight first, then torque in a crisscross pattern to load the gasket evenly.
